input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: COU Reynolds number crosses the laminar–turbulent transition the training set never spanned.

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts pressure drop at Re 4000 for a sizing decision

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Training is all laminar (Re ≤ 1500); the COU at Re 4000 is past the laminar–turbulent transition (~2300), a physically distinct regime with different dynamics the surrogate never trained on — not an interpolation.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the pressure-drop prediction is wrong (a verdict the regime gap does not establish)
treats the same-order Reynolds number as evidence of applicability across the transition

hardness.obvious_signal: the surrogate has low error (1.5%) and the COU Reynolds number is within the same order of magnitude as training
hardness.conflicting_signal: training is entirely laminar (Re ≤ 1500) but the COU is at Re 4000 — past the laminar–turbulent transition, a physically distinct regime with different governing dynamics the surrogate never saw

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 applicability: crossing the laminar–turbulent transition is a regime change, not interpolation; a laminar-trained surrogate is not applicable in turbulence.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
